passages.passage_text: Directions: Each question given below is followed by two arguments numbered I and II. You have to decide which of the arguments is a 'strong' argument and which is a 'weak' argument.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Should a total ban be put on trapping wild animals? Arguments: I. Yes. Trapping are making a lot of money. II. No. Bans on hunting and trapping are not effective.

choices.a: If only argument I is strong
choices.b: If only argument II is strong
choices.c: If either I or II is strong
choices.d: If neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: If both I and II strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Should there be compulsory military in our country? Arguments: I. No. It is against the policy of non-violence. II. Yes. Every citizen should protect his country.

choices.a: If only argument I is strong
choices.b: If only argument II is strong
choices.c: If either I or II is strong
choices.d: If neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: If both I and II strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Should all the remote parts of a country be connected by road? Arguments: I. No. It will disturb peaceful simple life of the villagers. II. Yes. It must be done immediately.

choices.a: If only argument I is strong
choices.b: If only argument II is strong
choices.c: If either I or II is strong
choices.d: If neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: If both I and II strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Should government jobs in rural areas be given more incentives? Arguments: I. No. Incentives are essential for attracting government servants there. II. No. Rural areas are already cheaper, healthier and less complex than big towns. So, why offer extra incentives?

choices.a: If only argument I is strong
choices.b: If only argument II is strong
choices.c: If either I or II is strong
choices.d: If neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: If both I and II strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Should religion be taught in our schools? Arguments: I. No. Ours is a secular state. II. Yes. Teaching religion helps inculcate moral values among children.

choices.a: If only argument I is strong
choices.b: If only argument II is strong
choices.c: If either I or II is strong
choices.d: If neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: If both I and II strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Should non-vegetarian food be totally banned in our country? Arguments: I. Yes. It is expensive and, therefore, beyond the reach of most of the people in our country. II. No. Nothing should be banned in a democratic country.

choices.a: If only argument I is strong
choices.b: If only argument II is strong
choices.c: If either I or II is strong
choices.d: If neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: If both I and II strong
